mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously when rates are in the accommodation zone.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.66% indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals sustained labor market strength and consumer income growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through increased corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS at 145,798 thousand suggests near-threshold employment levels
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with stable labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite strong payroll growth, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and forward returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) simultaneously signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline > 1.5% AND HOUST month-over-month decline > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS at 473,991M and HOUST at 1,246K units
regime_hint: demand-side recession or sharp demand shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances despite simultaneous RSAFS and HOUST contractions, or joint interaction term Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index below 104.0 (2017=100) signals supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate margin compression and reduced corporate profitability.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 104.5548 is near threshold
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO < 104.0 (mean return difference t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) above 245.0 combined with elevated federal funds rates above 1.5% signals monetary policy tightening in an inflationary environment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as real discount rates rise and growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:current CPIAUCSL at 244.456 and FEDFUNDS at 0.66%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when both conditions are met (t-test p > 0.10), or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rise of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite unemployment rise, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1,300 thousand units signals strong residential investment demand and forward-looking economic confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through multiplier effects on employment and consumer spending.
regime_hint: demand-side expansion with housing cycle strength
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive when HOUST growth > 3% (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust aggregate demand momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as corporate earnings expectations are upgraded in response to demand acceleration.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 16804.1B and RSAFS at 473,991M
regime_hint: strong cyclical expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when both conditions are met (t-test p > 0.10), or interaction Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
